title: crocanthemum arenicola
intro: Crocanthemum arenicola, also known as Beachy Sand Rosemary, is a flowering plant species native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent due to the presence of essential oils and terpenes in its leaves and stems. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Crocanthemum arenicola can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as added to salves and ointments for topical application.
